preferred_translation: delight is the root of dissatisfaction
alternative_translations: relishing is the root of dissatisfaction
delighting is the root of dissatisfaction
discouraged_translations: happiness is the root of suffering
definition: MN 1 closing causal line linking delighting to the root of dissatisfaction.
notes: Use this record for the closing causal turn of MN 1. It is a controlled full-line override of the nandī headword default: delight lands more cleanly than relishing in this concise causal sentence, while still preserving the active affective pull of the term.

example_phrases.translation: delight is the root of dissatisfaction
example_phrases.notes: Closing causal line in the Tathāgata section of MN 1.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the concise closing causal line that links delighting to the root of dukkha.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the MN 1 causal closing.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten delight into generic happiness or rotate back to suffering-language in this line.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the compact line from losing either the active nandī force or the repo's governed dukkha rendering.
